channel.title: Cork & Curve
channel.description: Wine and spirits travel guides, written by editors on the ground. Where the world drinks.

item.title: Alsace, France
item.description: Riesling, Gewurztraminer and 51 Grands Crus on the Vosges flank

item.title: Beaujolais, France
item.description: Ten granite crus, one grape, a natural-wine revolution

item.title: Bordeaux, France
item.description: Left Bank gravels, Right Bank clay, one world-class appellation

item.title: Burgundy, France
item.description: One thousand climats of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay

item.title: Champagne, France
item.description: The chalk cellars and Grand Cru villages of the world's benchmark sparkling wine

item.title: Douro Valley, Portugal
item.description: Schist terraces, fortified Port and a still-wine renaissance

item.title: Jerez & Sherry, Spain
item.description: Two DOs, three towns, one chalky terroir, flor yeast

item.title: Mosel, Germany
item.description: Riesling on slate, from feather-light Kabinett to noble Trockenbeerenauslese

item.title: Piedmont, Italy
item.description: Nebbiolo kings, Barbera workhorses, fog over the Langhe hills

item.title: Priorat, Spain
item.description: Garnacha, slate and the five pioneers who changed Spanish wine

item.title: Rhone Valley, France
item.description: Syrah, Viognier and Grenache across two hundred kilometres of the Rhone

item.title: Ribera del Duero, Spain
item.description: Tinto Fino on the Castilian plateau, from cooperative roots to cult-wine gravity

item.title: Rioja, Spain
item.description: Tempranillo, oak and the Ebro across three sub-zones

item.title: Sicily, Italy
item.description: Volcano, sea wind, and native varieties that change with every contrada

item.title: Tokaj, Hungary
item.description: Aszú gold from volcanic hillsides, 1737 royal decree, 2002 UNESCO inscription

item.title: Tuscany, Italy
item.description: Sangiovese hills, Super Tuscan coasts, one indelible terroir

item.title: Veneto, Italy
item.description: Amarone hills, Prosecco ridges, the full arc of Italian wine

item.title: Wachau, Austria
item.description: Danube terraces, gneiss soils, the Smaragd standard
